description: "A molecular function representing the directed movement of electrons from one molecular entity to another, typically mediated by electron carriers or acceptors, resulting in the transfer of energy and/or the reduction-oxidation (redox) transformation of chemical species. This activity is fundamental to various biological processes, including cellular respiration and photosynthesis, as well as numerous enzymatic reactions involved in metabolic pathways." [Wikipedia:Electron_transfer]
name: electron transfer activity

description: "Either of the lipid bilayers that surround the mitochondrion and form the mitochondrial envelope." [GOC:mah, NIF_Subcellular:sao1045389829]
name: mitochondrial membrane

description: "The double lipid bilayer enclosing the mitochondrion and separating its contents from the cell cytoplasm; includes the intermembrane space." [GOC:ai, GOC:pz]
name: mitochondrial envelope

description: "A process in which a series of electron carriers operate together to transfer electrons from donors to any of several different terminal electron acceptors." [GOC:mtg_electron_transport]
name: electron transport chain

description: "A double membrane structure enclosing an organelle, including two lipid bilayers and the region between them. In some cases, an organelle envelope may have more than two membranes." [GOC:mah, GOC:pz]
name: organelle envelope

description: "A membrane that is one of the two lipid bilayers of an organelle envelope or the outermost membrane of single membrane bound organelle." [GOC:dos, GOC:mah]
name: organelle membrane

description: "Enables the transfer of a proton from one side of a membrane to the other." [GOC:ai]
name: proton transmembrane transporter activity

description: "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of precursor metabolites, substances from which energy is derived, and any process involved in the liberation of energy from these substances." [GOC:jl]
name: generation of precursor metabolites and energy

description: "Enables the transfer of an ion from one side of a membrane to the other up the solute's concentration gradient. This is carried out by binding the solute and undergoing a series of conformational changes. Transport works equally well in either direction." [GOC:mtg_transport, ISBN:0815340729]
name: active monoatomic ion transmembrane transporter activity

description: "A semiautonomous, self replicating organelle that occurs in varying numbers, shapes, and sizes in the cytoplasm of virtually all eukaryotic cells. It is notably the site of tissue respiration." [GOC:giardia, ISBN:0198506732]
name: mitochondrion

description: "The inner, i.e. lumen-facing, lipid bilayer of an organelle envelope; usually highly selective to most ions and metabolites." [GOC:mah]
name: organelle inner membrane

description: "The contents of a cell excluding the plasma membrane and nucleus, but including other subcellular structures." [ISBN:0198547684]
name: cytoplasm

description: "Primary active transport of a solute across a membrane, driven by exothermic flow of electrons from a reduced substrate to an oxidized substrate. Primary active transport is catalysis of the transport of a solute across a membrane, up the solute's concentration gradient, by binding the solute and undergoing a series of conformational changes. Transport works equally well in either direction and is driven by a primary energy source." [GOC:mtg_transport, ISBN:0815340729, TC:3.D.-.-.-]
name: oxidoreduction-driven active transmembrane transporter activity

description: "The inner, i.e. lumen-facing, lipid bilayer of the mitochondrial envelope. It is highly folded to form cristae." [GOC:ai]
name: mitochondrial inner membrane

description: "A lipid bilayer along with all the proteins and protein complexes embedded in it and attached to it." [GOC:dos, GOC:mah, ISBN:0815316194]
name: membrane

description: "A component of a cell contained within (but not including) the plasma membrane. In eukaryotes it includes the nucleus and cytoplasm." [ISBN:0198506732]
name: intracellular anatomical structure

description: "Catalysis of an oxidation-reduction (redox) reaction in which a heme group acts as a hydrogen or electron donor and reduces a hydrogen or electron acceptor." [GOC:ai]
name: oxidoreductase activity, acting on a heme group of donors

description: "Catalysis of the reaction: 4 ferrocytochrome c + O2 + 4 H+ = 4 ferricytochrome c + 2 H2O." [RHEA:11436]
name: cytochrome-c oxidase activity

description: "The outer, i.e. cytoplasm-facing in a cellular organelle, lipid bilayer of an organelle envelope." [GOC:mah]
name: organelle outer membrane

description: "The external membrane of Gram-negative bacteria or certain organelles such as mitochondria and chloroplasts; freely permeable to most ions and metabolites." [GOC:go_curators]
name: outer membrane

description: "The chemical reactions and pathways involving the phosphate group, the anion or salt of any phosphoric acid." [GOC:ai]
name: phosphate-containing compound metabolic process

description: "The chemical reactions and pathways involving the nonmetallic element phosphorus or compounds that contain phosphorus." [GOC:ai]
name: phosphorus metabolic process

description: "Catalysis of the reactions: ATP + protein serine = ADP + protein serine phosphate." [RHEA:17989]
name: protein serine kinase activity

description: "The directed movement of substances (such as macromolecules, small molecules, ions) or cellular components (such as complexes and organelles) into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, or within a multicellular organism by means of some agent such as a transporter or a transporter complex, a pore or a motor protein." [GOC:dos, GOC:dph, GOC:jl, GOC:mah]
name: transport

description: "Any process that is carried out at the cellular level, but not necessarily restricted to a single cell. For example, cell communication occurs among more than one cell, but occurs at the cellular level." [GOC:go_curators, GOC:isa_complete]
name: cellular process

description: "The phosphorylation of ADP to ATP that accompanies the oxidation of a metabolite through the operation of the respiratory chain. Oxidation of compounds establishes a proton gradient across the membrane, providing the energy for ATP synthesis." [ISBN:0198506732, ISBN:0471331309]
name: oxidative phosphorylation

description: "The directed movement of a proton across a membrane." [GO_REF:0000069, GOC:pr, GOC:TermGenie]
name: proton transmembrane transport

description: "Enables the transfer of inorganic cations from one side of a membrane to the other. Inorganic cations are atoms or small molecules with a positive charge that do not contain carbon in covalent linkage." [GOC:ai, GOC:mtg_transport, ISBN:0815340729]
name: inorganic cation transmembrane transporter activity

description: "The region between the inner and outer lipid bilayers of the mitochondrial envelope." [GOC:mah]
name: mitochondrial intermembrane space

description: "Any protein complex that is part of a membrane." [GOC:dos]
name: membrane protein complex

description: "Any process that localizes a substance or cellular component. This may occur via movement, tethering or selective degradation." [GOC:ai, GOC:dos]
name: establishment of localization

description: Flower development initiation
name: Flower development initiation

description: "Enables the transfer of cation from one side of a membrane to the other." [GOC:dgf, GOC:mtg_transport, ISBN:0815340729]
name: monoatomic cation transmembrane transporter activity

description: "Enables the transfer of a solute from one side of a membrane to the other, up the solute's concentration gradient, by binding the solute and undergoing a series of conformational changes. Transport works equally well in either direction and is powered by a primary energy source. Primary energy sources known to be coupled to transport are chemical such as ATP hydrolysis, redox energy and photon energy." [GOC:mtg_transport, PMID:31613498]
name: primary active transmembrane transporter activity

description: "A process in which a series of electron carriers operate together to transfer electrons from donors such as NADH and FADH2 to any of several different terminal electron acceptors to generate a transmembrane electrochemical gradient." [GOC:mtg_electron_transport, ISBN:0716720094]
name: respiratory electron transport chain

description: "The region between the inner and outer lipid bilayers of an organelle envelope." [GOC:mah]
name: organelle envelope lumen

description: "Any process in which a cell, a substance, or a cellular entity, such as a protein complex or organelle, is transported, tethered to or otherwise maintained in a specific location. In the case of substances, localization may also be achieved via selective degradation." [GOC:ai, GOC:dos]
name: localization

description: "The lipid bilayer that forms the outer-most layer of an organelle." [GOC:dos]
name: bounding membrane of organelle

description: "Enables the transfer of an ion from one side of a membrane to the other." [GOC:dgf, GOC:mtg_transport, ISBN:0815340729]
name: monoatomic ion transmembrane transporter activity

description: "Any protein complex that is part of a respiratory chain." [GOC:dos]
name: respiratory chain complex

description: "A membranous cellular structure that bears the photosynthetic pigments in plants, algae, and cyanobacteria. In cyanobacteria thylakoids are of various shapes and are attached to, or continuous with, the plasma membrane. In eukaryotes they are flattened, membrane-bounded disk-like structures located in the chloroplasts; in the chloroplasts of higher plants the thylakoids form dense stacks called grana. Isolated thylakoid preparations can carry out photosynthetic electron transport and the associated phosphorylation." [GOC:ds, GOC:mtg_sensu, ISBN:0198506732]
name: thylakoid

description: "Sac-like membranous structures (cisternae) in a chloroplast combined into stacks (grana) and present singly in the stroma (stroma thylakoids or frets) as interconnections between grana. An example of this component is found in Arabidopsis thaliana." [GOC:mtg_sensu, ISBN:0943088399]
name: chloroplast thylakoid

description: "Any thylakoid within a plastid." [GOC:pz]
name: plastid thylakoid

description: "A discrete extra-nucleolar subnuclear domain, 20-50 in number, in which splicing factors are seen to be localized by immunofluorescence microscopy." [PMID:28977640]
name: nuclear speck

description: "Enables the transfer of a specific substance or related group of substances from one side of a membrane to the other, up the solute's concentration gradient. The transporter binds the solute and undergoes a series of conformational changes. Transport works equally well in either direction." [GOC:mtg_transport, ISBN:0815340729]
name: active transmembrane transporter activity

description: "Organized structure of distinctive morphology and function, bounded by a single or double lipid bilayer membrane. Includes the nucleus, mitochondria, plastids, vacuoles, and vesicles. Excludes the plasma membrane." [GOC:go_curators]
name: membrane-bounded organelle

description: "A part of a cellular organism consisting of a material entity with granularity above the level of a protein complex but below that of an anatomical system. Note that cellular organisms exclude viruses." [GOC:kmv]
name: cellular anatomical structure

description: "The double lipid bilayer enclosing a plastid and separating its contents from the rest of the cytoplasm; includes the intermembrane space." [GOC:jy]
name: plastid envelope

description: "Organized structure of distinctive morphology and function. Includes the nucleus, mitochondria, plastids, vacuoles, vesicles, ribosomes and the cytoskeleton, and prokaryotic structures such as anammoxosomes and pirellulosomes. Excludes the plasma membrane." [GOC:go_curators]
name: organelle

description: "Enables the transfer of an inorganic molecular entity from the outside of a cell to the inside of the cell across a membrane. An inorganic molecular entity is a molecular entity that contains no carbon." [GOC:mtg_transport, ISBN:0815340729]
name: inorganic molecular entity transmembrane transporter activity

description: "Catalysis of the reactions: ATP + protein serine = ADP + protein serine phosphate, and ATP + protein threonine = ADP + protein threonine phosphate." [EC:2.7.11.-, GOC:bf, PMID:2956925]
name: protein serine/threonine kinase activity

description: "Catalysis of an oxidation-reduction (redox) reaction in which NADH or NADPH acts as a hydrogen or electron donor and reduces a hydrogen or electron acceptor." [GOC:ai]
name: oxidoreductase activity, acting on NAD(P)H

description: "The chemical reactions and pathways involving ATP, adenosine triphosphate, a universally important coenzyme and enzyme regulator." [GOC:go_curators]
name: ATP metabolic process

description: "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of ATP driven by transport of protons across a membrane to generate an electrochemical gradient (proton-motive force)." [GOC:vw]
name: proton motive force-driven ATP synthesis

description: "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of ATP, adenosine 5'-triphosphate, a universally important coenzyme and enzyme regulator." [GOC:go_curators, ISBN:0198506732]
name: ATP biosynthetic process
